title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Set 10: Mixed Review
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the result of 406 - 178 + 92?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='200' height='70' viewBox='0 0 200 70'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='50' height='30'/><rect x='75' y='20' width='50' height='30'/><rect x='140' y='20' width='50' height='30'/></g><text x='22' y='42' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>406</text><text x='62' y='42' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>-</text><text x='87' y='42' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>178</text><text x='127' y='42' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>+</text><text x='152' y='42' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>92</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three boxed numbers showing the calculation 406 - 178 + 92.

questions.question_text: A rectangle measures 9 cm by 4 cm. What is its area?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='180' height='100' viewBox='0 0 180 100'><rect x='20' y='20' width='135' height='60' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='75' y='15' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>9 cm</text><text x='160' y='55' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>4 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle labeled 9 cm by 4 cm.
questions.solution: Area = length x width = 9 x 4 = 36 cm^2.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 6:45 PM and lasts 1 hour 40 minutes. At what time does it end?
questions.solution: 6:45 PM + 1 hour = 7:45 PM. 7:45 PM + 40 minutes = 8:25 PM.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is divisible by both 3 and 4?
questions.solution: A number divisible by both 3 and 4 must be divisible by 12. Among the choices, 36 = 12 x 3.

questions.question_text: What number comes next in the pattern: 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, ...?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='260' height='60' viewBox='0 0 260 60'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='115' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='205' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='245' cy='30' r='8' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/></g><text x='18' y='35' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>4</text><text x='63' y='35' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>9</text><text x='106' y='35' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>16</text><text x='151' y='35' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>25</text><text x='196' y='35' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>36</text><text x='240' y='34' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Sequence of circles with the numbers 4, 9, 16, 25, 36 and a dashed circle with a question mark.
questions.solution: The sequence is 2^2, 3^2, 4^2, 5^2, 6^2, so the next term is 7^2 = 49.

questions.question_text: A jug holds 2.5 liters of juice. How many milliliters is that?
questions.solution: 1 liter = 1000 mL, so 2.5 L = 2500 mL.

questions.question_text: How many small squares (of side 1) fit inside the grid shown?
questions.image_description: A rectangle divided into a 4 by 3 grid.
questions.solution: The grid has 4 columns and 3 rows, giving 4 x 3 = 12 unit squares.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 60 cents. How much do 7 pencils cost?
Four friends run a race. Maya finishes before Noah. Noah finishes before Priya. Priya finishes before Quinn. Who finishes last?
questions.solution: The order from first to last is Maya, Noah, Priya, Quinn, so Quinn finishes last.

questions.question_text: What is one quarter of 84?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='140' height='140' viewBox='0 0 140 140'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='55'/><line x1='70' y1='15' x2='70' y2='125'/><line x1='15' y1='70' x2='125' y2='70'/></g><text x='62' y='45' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>1/4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A circle divided into four equal quarters.
questions.solution: One quarter of 84 is 84 / 4 = 21.

questions.question_text: If 4n - 7 = 25, what is the value of n?
The figure below is made of five identical small squares. If the total area is 45 cm^2, what is the side length of one small square?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='200' height='120' viewBox='0 0 200 120'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='50' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='80' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='110' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='80' y='10' width='30' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A plus-like shape made of 5 equal squares.
questions.solution: One small square has area 45 / 5 = 9 cm^2, so its side is sqrt(9) = 3 cm.

questions.question_text: In a class, the ratio of boys to girls is 3:5. If there are 24 students in total, how many are boys?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='260' height='50' viewBox='0 0 260 50'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='5' y='15' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='35' y='15' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='65' y='15' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='110' y='15' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='140' y='15' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='170' y='15' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='200' y='15' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='230' y='15' width='25' height='25'/></g><text x='30' y='12' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>boys</text><text x='175' y='12' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>girls</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three boxes representing boys and five boxes representing girls, showing the 3 to 5 ratio.
questions.solution: The ratio has 3 + 5 = 8 parts. Each part is 24 / 8 = 3 students. Boys = 3 x 3 = 9.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of the first five positive odd numbers?
Tom has 3 different shirts and 4 different pairs of pants. How many different outfits (one shirt and one pair of pants) can he make?
questions.solution: By the multiplication principle, 3 x 4 = 12 outfits.

questions.question_text: In a tile arrangement each row has 2 more tiles than the previous, and row 1 has 3 tiles. How many tiles are in row 6?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='220' height='120' viewBox='0 0 220 120'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='90' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='90' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='110' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='130' y='80' width='20' height='20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Rows of squares: row 1 has 3, row 2 has 5, row 3 has 7.
questions.solution: Row n has 3 + 2(n-1) tiles. Row 6 has 3 + 2(5) = 13 tiles.

questions.question_text: A water tank leaks 15 mL every minute. How many liters does it lose in one hour?
questions.solution: In 60 minutes: 60 x 15 = 900 mL = 0.9 L.

questions.question_text: A triangle has angles of 40 degrees and 75 degrees. What is the third angle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='180' height='140' viewBox='0 0 180 140'><polygon points='30,110 150,110 70,30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='35' y='105' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>40</text><text x='135' y='105' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>75</text><text x='65' y='50' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Triangle with two marked angles: 40 and 75 degrees.
questions.solution: Angles sum to 180 degrees: 180 - 40 - 75 = 65 degrees.

questions.question_text: What is the value of the expression 24 / (6 - 2) + 5 x 2?

Five cards labeled 1 to 5 lie in a row. Card 3 is to the right of card 1. Card 5 is to the left of card 2. Card 4 is between card 1 and card 5. Card 1 is the leftmost. In which position from the left is card 2?
questions.solution: The full order left to right is 1, 4, 5, 2, 3. Card 2 is in the 4th position.

questions.question_text: What is the smallest positive integer greater than 1 that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 2, 3, 4, and 5?
questions.solution: The number minus 1 must be divisible by 2, 3, 4, and 5. LCM(2,3,4,5) = 60, so the smallest such number is 60 + 1 = 61.

questions.question_text: A large square of side 12 cm is divided by its two diagonals into four triangles. What is the area of one of these triangles?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='160' height='160' viewBox='0 0 160 160'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='120' height='120'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='140' y2='140'/><line x1='140' y1='20' x2='20' y2='140'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with both diagonals drawn, forming four triangles.
questions.solution: Total area = 12 x 12 = 144 cm^2. The diagonals split it into 4 equal triangles, each 144 / 4 = 36 cm^2.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive whole numbers is 96. What is the largest of the three numbers?
questions.solution: If the numbers are n-1, n, n+1, their sum is 3n = 96, so n = 32. The largest is 33.

questions.question_text: How many 3-digit numbers can be formed using the digits 1, 2, 3, 4 if no digit is repeated?
questions.solution: Choices: 4 x 3 x 2 = 24.

questions.question_text: On an island, knights always tell the truth and knaves always lie. Three islanders A, B, C make statements. A says: 'B is a knave.' B says: 'C is a knight.' C says: 'A and B are of different types.' How many knights are there among A, B, and C?
questions.solution: Case 1: A is a knight. Then B is a knave (lying), so C is not a knight (a knave). Then C lies, meaning A and B are the same type - contradiction. Case 2: A is a knave. Then B is actually a knight. B truthfully says C is a knight. C truthfully says A and B differ (knave vs knight) - consistent. So B and C are knights while A is a knave, giving exactly 2 knights.

questions.question_text: A cube is painted red on all sides and then cut into 27 identical small cubes (3 x 3 x 3). How many small cubes have exactly two red faces?
questions.image_description: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube viewed in perspective, divided into 27 small cubes.
questions.solution: Small cubes with exactly two red faces are the edge cubes (not at corners). A cube has 12 edges, and each edge contributes 1 such cube in a 3 x 3 x 3, giving 12.

questions.question_text: What is the units digit of 7^15?
questions.solution: Units digits of 7^n cycle: 7, 9, 3, 1, 7, 9, 3, 1, ... with period 4. Since 15 mod 4 = 3, the units digit is 3.

questions.question_text: A recipe requires flour and sugar in the ratio 5:2. If Lina uses 350 g of flour, how many grams of sugar does she need?
questions.solution: Each 'part' is 350 / 5 = 70 g. Sugar = 2 x 70 = 140 g.

questions.question_text: A sequence begins 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21, ... where each term after the second is the sum of the previous two. What is the 10th term?
questions.solution: Continuing: 13, 21, 34, 55. The 10th term is 55.

questions.question_text: A car travels at a constant 72 km per hour. How many meters does it travel in 5 seconds?
